rationale: The one substantive GT insight (yJader's inline comment) is that removing `already_submitted` is correct because StagePool rejects list prompts in both submit_initial and submit_update, so unwrapping shouldn't depend on that flag. Y independently verifies exactly this by reading stage_pool.py's ValueError raises in both paths, arriving at the same reasoning and correctly recommending APPROVE (matching the actual unanimous LGTM outcome). X instead flags the multi-element fall-through as a 'major' silent-failure bug requiring a new elif/ValueError, but its own evidence quotes the PR description saying such lists are already rejected downstream — contradicting itself and missing that this is precisely the behavior GT confirms is intentional and sufficient, leading X to an unwarranted FOLLOW-UP-REQUIRED verdict. X's remaining findings (defensive assert, generic test-coverage note) are speculative rather than grounded bugs, dragging down precision despite decent actionability.
